_meta.description: Screen 2 universe — mid-cap names selected for pre-earnings filings readability. A name earns a slot only if a careful read of its 10-K, 10-Q, and recent 8-K earnings exhibits can plausibly front-run the earnings print.

constraints.commercial_stage_only: no pre-revenue / clinical-stage names; their catalyst is trial readouts, not earnings prints

notes: CAKE: deliberate dividend exception (~2.5% yield, above the 1.5% threshold) — kept per owner override. Do NOT auto-drop on rule sweeps.

DCO: market cap sits just above the $2B floor (~$2.1B as of May 2026) after a sharp run-up. Valid today but the most fragile cap in the universe — a modest pullback drops it out of band. Do NOT auto-drop on a single rule sweep without review.
ADR / foreign-issuer names (ARGX, SIMO, DLO, QGEN, BIRK): flagged in rationale — verify trading liquidity before relying on fills.
datacenter_buildout subcategory (POWL, AAON, MOD, BDC): capex picks-and-shovels cluster; doubles as the seed for the future Screen 4.
Universe selected to avoid overlap with Screen 1's AI_ADJACENT_UNIVERSE (vertical-workflow / healthcare-IT / cybersecurity SaaS) so the two screens' grading buckets stay uncorrelated.

tickers.rationale: Cloud usage, search/observability demand, and expansion trends are readable through ARR and consumption commentary.
Atlas growth, consumption, and enterprise expansion make the quarterly print readable.
Seat expansion, enterprise mix, and billings trends give useful pre-print signals.
Enterprise penetration, retention, and sales efficiency create readable quarterly variance.
Construction volume and customer expansion show up clearly in revenue and billings.
SMB/customer-support demand, NRR, and sales efficiency are visible quarter to quarter.
Property-management usage and payments monetization make the print operationally readable.
Renewal, upsell, and operating leverage trends are visible in billings and margin.
Seat growth, enterprise adoption, and RPO provide strong earnings-readability.
Cloud migration and usage growth are visible in cohort and revenue metrics.
Transaction volume, payment activity, and SMB health make earnings readable. Placed in software (not fintech) bucket; do not double-count.
Cloud ARR transition and large-enterprise deployment timing create readable variance.
Product-level rare-disease sales and reimbursement trends are readable.
Cabometyx sales, royalties, and oncology demand provide clean quarterly drivers.
Duchenne therapy uptake and patient-start trends are visible in the print.
CAPLYTA prescription growth and payer access make earnings readable.
Commercial launch uptake and reimbursement access create readable quarterly signals.
ADR, verify liquidity; Vyvgart utilization and geographic expansion drive the print.
Product mix and prescription trends give clear quarter-to-quarter signals.
Lybalvi/Vivitrol sales and royalty economics are readable in filings.
Commercial CNS prescription trends and launch uptake create readable earnings variance.
Rare-disease product uptake and patient growth are visible in quarterly revenue.
Nuplazid/Daybue utilization and payer dynamics drive the print.
Consumer-health mix, retailer inventory, and margin commentary are readable.
Backlog conversion and carrier/cloud spending create readable quarterly setups.
Industrial inventory correction and order recovery are visible in bookings.
Timing-chip demand across datacenter/comms creates readable segment signals.
Auto/industrial inventory trends show up in channel and backlog commentary.
Memory-interface and datacenter demand trends are visible in licensing/product mix.
Telecom, defense, and industrial RF demand are readable through segment trends.
Ion-implantation backlog and capex commentary create strong print-readability.
ADR, verify liquidity; NAND/controller inventory cycles are readable.
Industrial/appliance inventory correction shows up in order and margin trends.
Datacenter and telecom optics demand plus inventory-cycle dynamics are readable in segment and customer-mix commentary. Replaced COHR (over the $20B cap ceiling).
Member growth, lending mix, deposits, and NIM make earnings readable.
GMV, delinquency, take rate, and funding appetite drive the print.
Loan conversion, approval rates, and funding partner activity are visible.
Education/healthcare payment volume and take rate are readable.
TPV, customer concentration, and card-program activity drive variance.
ADR, verify liquidity; cross-border volume and take rate are readable.
Restaurant/hospitality payment volume and gateway activity show up in the print.
SMB payment volume, take rate, and geography mix are readable.
Drone backlog, contract timing, and defense demand are filings-readable.
Defense-systems backlog and book-to-bill create readable earnings setups.
Defense electronics recovery, backlog, and margin execution are readable.
Defense electronics, sensing, and backlog trends make the print readable.
Contract awards, backlog, and defense/intelligence demand drive earnings.
Federal contract flow, bookings, and margin mix are readable.
Government services backlog and book-to-bill provide quarterly signals.
Bell/Textron Aviation backlog and delivery cadence are readable.
Parts, MRO demand, and defense/commercial aviation mix drive the print.

Aerostructures and missile-franchise components with a real disclosed backlog; multi-quarter margin progression (VISION 2027) is readable in the print. NOTE: cap (~$2.1B) sits just above the $2B floor — most fragile cap in the universe, do not auto-drop on a single sweep. Replaced RKLB (over the $20B cap ceiling).
Comps, unit growth, and franchise margins are highly readable.
Traffic, pricing, and restaurant-level margins show up clearly.

DIVIDEND EXCEPTION (~2.5% yield, above 1.5% threshold) — kept per owner override, do not auto-drop. Traffic, pricing, and margin pressure are readable; lower-middle-income discretionary exposure makes consumer weakness visible early.
Wholesale inventory, HEYDUDE trends, and DTC mix drive the print.
Premium discretionary demand and channel mix are readable.
Mall traffic, promotions, and inventory levels create readable variance.
Topgolf traffic and equipment sell-through are operationally visible.
Product cycles and channel inventory drive earnings reactions.
Housing-sensitive luxury demand and order cadence are readable.
Foreign issuer, verify liquidity; premium footwear demand and wholesale/DTC mix are readable.
Switchgear backlog, bookings, and project cadence make the print highly readable.
HVAC/cooling backlog and margin conversion are strong quarterly drivers.
Data-center thermal-management demand and segment margins are readable.
Mission-critical cabling/connectivity demand and orders are readable.
Electrical conduit, construction exposure, pricing, and volume trends are visible.
Aircraft leasing and utilization trends are readable.
Pricing, landfill volume, and regional demand drive the print.
OEM/replacement demand and margin mix are readable.
Retailer inventory and consumer battery demand are visible.
Water systems and infrastructure order trends are readable.
Instrument bookings and pharma/academic capex are readable.
Instrument placements and consumables pull-through drive the print.
Biotech funding, bookings, and lab demand create readable variance; recent guidance cuts and margin moves are a textbook readable print.
Reagent/diagnostics mix and end-market demand are visible.
ADR, verify liquidity; molecular-testing and consumables demand are readable.
Test volume, reimbursement, and oncology adoption drive earnings.

Commercial test-volume growth, reimbursement trends, and oncology/prenatal mix make the print readable via volume and payer dynamics; operationally driven rather than binary-clinical. Replaced EXAS (cap near/over the $20B ceiling).
Procedure volume, logistics revenue, and transplant-center adoption are visible.
